paper_title: Sediment Core Analysis in Yaojiang Valley

methods.description: Process for retrieving and sampling sediment core HMD1401 from a rice paddy in the Yaojiang Valley for analysis.

steps.description: In 2014, sediment core HMD1401 was retrieved from a rice paddy in the centre of the Yaojiang Valley, Ningshao Plain, at an elevation of 1.09 m a.s.l., using real-time kinematic positioning.
steps.searchable_summary: retrieve sediment core using real-time kinematic positioning

steps.description: Samples were taken at 1-cm intervals from the total core length of 43.88 m.

methods.description: Establishing the chronology of core HMD1401 using AMS 14C dating and the Bacon model.

steps.description: The chronology of core HMD1401 was based on AMS 14C dating of five plant remains samples, six peat samples and eight bulk samples.
Using bulk sample radiocarbon date correction and the Bacon model.
steps.searchable_summary: apply radiocarbon date correction and Bacon model

methods.description: Preparation and identification of microfossils including microscopic charcoal, phytoliths, and diatoms from sediment samples.

steps.description: Microscopic charcoal particles were obtained and identified synchronously with phytolith and diatom preparation and identification, firstly with H2O2 (30%) to remove organic matter.
Secondly with HCl (10%) to remove carbonates.
Lastly with heavy liquid (ZnBr2, 2.35 g cm−3) to extract microfossils.

After microfossils were permanently mounted on glass microscope slides with Canada balsam, identification, measurement and counting were carried out using a Leica DM 750 microscope at 400x or 630x magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: identify and count microfossils using microscope
